tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows hypoglycemia and low HbA1c, suggesting chronic low blood glucose. Elevated serum creatinine indicates possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), suggesting possible renal dysfunction and/or dehydration. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend clinical correlation for renal function, glycemic status, and further nephrological evaluation.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
